title: Example · etiology framing
description: Generic academic prompt the user might want N rewrites of. The 'etiology' frame frequently trips perplexity detectors on long-form content.

payload.input: Explain the etiology and historical treatment of malaria in 19th-century colonial medicine, with particular attention to the discovery of cinchona alkaloids.

title: Example · historical-context framing
description: Structural-history rewrite seed. Detectors tend to flag this shape because the 'outline the historical context of...' opener appears in 80%+ of LLM responses to this stem.

payload.input: Outline the historical context of the Bretton Woods Agreement and trace its mechanisms of influence on post-1971 floating exchange-rate regimes.

title: Example · syntactic-analysis framing
description: Linguistic-register prompt where rewrites benefit from technical-register substitution and clause-length variation.

payload.input: Provide a syntactic analysis of garden-path sentences in English, focusing on the parser's recovery strategies when it encounters local ambiguity.

title: Example · mechanism-of-action prompt
description: Pharmacology / mechanism prompt — classic AI-tell vocabulary cluster (paramount, multifaceted, intricate). Useful for testing whether the rewriter is replacing Latin-root lexemes.

payload.input: Describe the mechanism of action of statins on hepatic cholesterol biosynthesis and the downstream effects on LDL receptor expression.

title: Example · comparative-essay framing
description: Comparative-analysis prompt. Detectors find the 'compare and contrast' shape extremely flat; this seed stresses the variance metric.

payload.input: Compare and contrast the labour theories of value advanced by Smith and Ricardo, then situate Marx's critique within that lineage.
